provisions.description: Requires the President, notwithstanding any other provision of law, to enter into an arrangement with Ukraine within 60 days of enactment for the repayment of economic assistance provided under the Economic Support Fund and Assistance for Europe, Eurasia and Central Asia headings in Title IV of this division. Terms to be set by the President. Prohibits cancellation of indebtedness before November 15, 2024. After January 1, 2026, allows cancellation of remaining indebtedness subject to congressional review.

provisions.description: Establishes congressional review procedures for Presidential actions related to the cancellation of Ukraine indebtedness under section 507. Requires the President to submit a written report to Congress before taking action. Sets review periods of 10 calendar days (2024) or 30 calendar days (2025 onward, 60 days if submitted July 10-September 7). Provides expedited joint resolution of disapproval procedures for both the House and Senate, including committee discharge, floor consideration, debate limits, and coordination between chambers.

provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text: Section 2606 of the Ukraine Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2022 (division N of Public Law 117–103) is amended as follows: (1) in subsection (a), by
notes: Subsection (a)(1) expands eligibility to major non-NATO allies and the Indo-Pacific region, doubles the direct loan ceiling from $4B to $8B, and removes minimum interest rate requirement.
Subsection (a)(2) expands loan guarantee eligibility similarly, doubles the guarantee ceiling from $4B to $8B, and adds exception for FFB-guaranteed loans.

Subsection (b) limits use of funds for non-NATO allies and Indo-Pacific to FMFP funds from this division and prior State/foreign ops acts; requires Secretary of State certification 15 days prior to obligation and consultation with committees 60 days after enactment.
Subsection (c) emergency designation for repurposed amounts under section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985.
